panels.title: Forward-leg JetStream publish failures
panels.description: cdc_forward_publish_failed: publishes that errored and were NACKed back to the redis_streams input for replay (no ack, no loss). Nonzero means NATS is rejecting/unreachable while the source keeps retrying. SHARDED render (subject-sharding v2): the reject fallback (and this counter) is removed — publish failures retry IN PLACE and surface as output_error on the connect-source job instead (second series; drives CDCForwardPublishBlocked).

panels.title: Connect processing latency p50/p95/p99 (s)
panels.description: Connect timing histograms (use_histogram_timing: true). Metric name says _ns but the histogram buckets record SECONDS in this Connect build — treat values as seconds.

panels.title: End-to-end CDC latency percentiles (s)
panels.description: cdc_latency_seconds from the latency-calculator (region XADD timestamp delta, rolling window). Its job label differs from the connect legs, so this panel scopes by namespace only.

panels.title: Writer throughput & errors (ops/s)
panels.description: cdc_writer_* from the writer's own job (scoped by namespace only — the $job variable tracks the connect legs).

panels.title: Elector leadership & lease ops
panels.description: elector_leading per pod (1 = holds the Lease; exactly one per leg) and stream POST/DELETE error rate. Scoped by namespace only.

panels.title: Sink apply throughput by group (job) — ops/s
panels.description: Per-group apply rate. With multi-subject sink groups each group is a distinct Prometheus job (its Service name, e.g. lab-connect-sink / lab-connect-sink-a), so `by (job)` breaks throughput out per group; a single-group install shows one series.

panels.title: Forward-leg unrouted (parked)
panels.description: cdc_forward_unrouted by reason: empty_prefix = no derivable key segment (malformed event); no_match = key matched no configured prefix AND no catchAll group is deployed — either way the event publishes to kv.cdc.others.<op>, which nothing consumes, and parks in the stream. Only nonzero when prefix routing is configured. Drives the CDCForwardUnrouted alert. Fix the key format, add a sinkGroup for the missing prefix, or deploy a catchAll group.

panels.title: Forward-leg catch-all traffic (others)
panels.description: cdc_forward_others: events whose key matched no configured prefix, published to kv.cdc.others.<op> and consumed by the catchAll sink group (reason=no_match). Emitted ONLY when a catchAll group is deployed — without one the same miss increments cdc_forward_unrouted (panel 11) and alerts. A sustained rate here means a key family is flowing through the catch-all: consider giving it its own sinkGroup.

panels.title: Sync latency p50/p95/p99 (s) by group
panels.description: cdc_sync_latency_seconds: writer mint (envelope ts) → sink successful apply, ALL ops — includes forward leg, JetStream, and redelivery/replay time. Timing is fed in ns; the exporter divides by 1e9, so values are SECONDS. sum by (le, job): each sink group keeps its own distribution — merging jobs would produce a bogus blended quantile.

panels.title: Sync clock-skew negatives
panels.description: cdc_sync_skew_negative: messages whose writer ts was AHEAD of the sink clock (delta < 0). The latency sample is clamped to 0 (the metric processor rejects negatives), and counted here instead. Non-zero ⇒ writer clock ahead of sink (NTP drift) — investigate. Corrupt or missing ts never reaches this counter: it is caught by the ts parse guard (sync_has_ts=no) and skipped entirely.

panels.title: Sharded apply throughput by shard (ops/s)
panels.description: cdc_apply rate per shard (subject-sharding v2). Only sharded sink groups emit the shard label. Watch for balance: a hot shard (hot employee) saturates its serialized 1/RTT fetch lane while others idle — drives the CDCShardSkew alert. shard=sx is the isolation lane and belongs at zero (panel 16).

panels.title: Shard isolation lane (must stay 0)
panels.description: Sharding invariant signals (design v2 §11 A3): cross_shard_rename = a rename whose old/new keys resolve to different shards (INV-S7 — any nonzero value is a bug); unparseable_shard = a sharded-family key without a keyPattern id (key contract broken); sx applies = traffic through the isolation shard. All three route to sx so nothing is lost — but nonzero means the key contract or the shard function is broken. NOTE: v2 has NO direct out-of-order signal (no fence) — these are proxies. Drives the CDCShardIsolationLane alert.

panels.title: Sync latency p99 (s) by shard
panels.description: cdc_sync_latency_seconds p99 per shard (sharded groups only; buckets are SECONDS). A single shard's p99 climbing while others stay flat = that shard's serialized lane is saturated or blocked (a poison message under max_ack_pending=1 blocks the whole shard — see CDCShardStuck). Companion of the CDCSyncLatencyHigh alert.
